tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significant renal impairment (markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, microalbuminuria), hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, and metabolic acidosis (low CO₂). Urinalysis reveals nephrotic-range proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). LDL cholesterol is mildly elevated. Findings are suggestive of advanced kidney disease, likely with nephrotic syndrome and possible underlying diabetes with poor glycemic control. Immediate nephrology and endocrinology evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
